steps.title: Identify what the passage needs
steps.content:
The passage gives Kamoku-Kapulena's area (7.47 square miles) as one example of a home land much smaller than Homuula-Upper Piihonua, then starts a second example: "the total area of Nanakuli is ______". The blank must be filled with Nanakuli's actual area from the table — no calculation or inference needed, just reading the correct row.
  • Choice A — 37.26 square miles.: This is the area of a different home land from the table, not Nanakuli. Does not complete the Nanakuli example. ✗
  • Choice B — 3.61 square miles.: This is Nanakuli's area as shown in the table. It is smaller than the largest home land and completes the passage's second concrete example. ✓
  • Choice C — 23.57 square miles.: This is the area of a different home land from the table, not Nanakuli. Does not complete the Nanakuli example. ✗
  • Choice D — 15.99 square miles.: This is the area of a different home land from the table, not Nanakuli. Does not complete the Nanakuli example. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: The table shows Nanakuli's area as 3.61 square miles — a value clearly smaller than Homuula-Upper Piihonua's ~100 square miles, consistent with the passage's point. B is correct.
